Title: User Login Success
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email and password.
3. Click the login button.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully and redirected to the home page.

Title: User Login Failure - Invalid Email
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the invalid email and valid password.
3. Click the login button.
Expected Result: Error message displayed indicating invalid email format.

Title: User Login Failure - Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email and incorrect password.
3. Click the login button.
Expected Result: Error message displayed indicating incorrect password.

Title: User Logout
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the user profile page.
2. Click the logout button.
Expected Result: User is logged out and redirected to the login page.

Title: Create Post Success
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Title: "My First Post", Content: "This is my first post."
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the post creation page.
2. Enter the title and content.
3. Click the submit button.
Expected Result: Post is created successfully and displayed on the user's feed.

Title: Delete Post Success
Preconditions: User has an existing post.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the post details page.
2. Click the delete button.
3. Confirm the deletion.
Expected Result: Post is deleted successfully and removed from the user's feed.

2. Click the add user button.
3. Select the user to add.
4. Confirm the addition.
Expected Result: User is added to the neighborhood successfully.

Title: Remove User from Neighborhood Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and a neighborhood exists.
Test Steps:

2. Click the remove user button.
3. Select the user to remove.
4. Confirm the removal.
Expected Result: User is removed from the neighborhood successfully.

Title: User Creates Admin Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has admin creation permissions.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the admin creation page.
2. Enter the username and password.
3. Click the create button.
Expected Result: Admin is created successfully and added to the system.

Title: User Edits Admin Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has admin editing permissions.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the admin details page.
2. Click the edit button.
3. Enter the new password.
4. Click the save button.
Expected Result: Admin details are updated successfully.

Title: User Deletes Admin Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has admin deletion permissions.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the admin details page.
2. Click the delete button.
3. Confirm the deletion.
Expected Result: Admin is deleted successfully and removed from the system.

Title: User Gets Map Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a map.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the map details page.
2. Click the get map button.
Expected Result: Map is retrieved successfully and displayed.

Title: User Adds Map Success
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Name: "New Map", Description: "A new map for the user."
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the map creation page.
2. Enter the name and description.
3. Click the add map button.
Expected Result: Map is added successfully and displayed in the user's maps list.

Title: User Gets Profile Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a profile.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile details page.
2. Click the get profile button.
Expected Result: Profile is retrieved successfully and displayed.

Title: User Updates Profile Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a profile.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile details page.
2. Click the update profile button.
3. Enter the new name and description.
4. Click the save button.
Expected Result: Profile is updated successfully and displayed with the new information.

Title: Navigate from General Dashboard to Specific Section and Back
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the general dashboard.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the general dashboard.
2. Click on a section link.
3. Navigate to a specific section within the selected section.
4. Click the back button to return to the general dashboard.
Expected Result: User navigates successfully from the general dashboard to a specific section and back.

Title: User Creates New Entry Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has permission to create entries.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the entry creation page.
2. Click the create new entry button.
Expected Result: User is prompted to validate credentials.

Title: User Validates Credentials Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has permission to create entries.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the entry creation page.
2. Click the validate credentials button.
Expected Result: User is authorized to proceed.

Title: User Authenticates Success
Preconditions: User is logged in and has permission to create entries.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the entry creation page.
2. Click the authenticate button.
Expected Result: User is authenticated successfully and allowed to create the entry.

Title: User Creates New Entry Failure - Unauthorized
Preconditions: User is logged in but lacks permission to create entries.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the entry creation page.
2. Click the create new entry button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating lack of authorization.

Title: User Validates Credentials Failure - Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User is logged in but provides invalid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the entry creation page.
2. Click the validate credentials button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating invalid credentials.

Title: User Authenticates Failure - Authentication Failed
Preconditions: User is logged in but fails authentication.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the entry creation page.
2. Click the authenticate button.
Expected Result: User receives an error message indicating authentication failure.
